summary: Matched and linked the event Yoshi Egg item object by implementing key code/data in `src/melee/it/items/itevyoshiegg.c`, adding EvYoshiEgg-specific item vars/dat attrs, renaming identified symbols, and switching the object from `NonMatching` to `Matching`. The decomp-dev bot reported 5 new matches: `itEvYoshiEgg_Logic42_DmgReceived`, `itEvYoshiEgg_Spawn`, `.data`, `itEvyoshiegg_UnkMotion5_Anim`, and `.sdata2`.

key_files.role: Main implementation: added item state table, spawn function, motion-5 animation, damage-received logic, and EvYoshiEgg item-var accesses.
Updated item logic table to call `itEvYoshiEgg_Logic42_DmgReceived`.
Kept the preserved/commented logic-table reference in sync with the renamed damage callback.
Renamed matched functions and converted related `.sdata2` labels from global `it_804DCCxx` names to local compiler-style `@` labels.

what_changed: Implemented `itEvYoshiEgg_Spawn(Vec3*)`, constructing a `SpawnItem` for `It_Kind_EvYoshiEgg`, copying position into `prev_pos` and `pos`, setting facing/velocity/parent/flag fields, spawning with `Item_80268B18`, then initializing the item through `it_8029B268` and resetting `evyoshiegg.xDD4`.
Added `ItemStateTable it_803F6728` with six EvYoshiEgg state entries using the existing `itEvyoshiegg_UnkMotionN_{Anim,Phys,Coll}` callbacks.

Implemented `itEvyoshiegg_UnkMotion5_Anim`: checks `evyoshiegg.xDD8`, calls `gm_801BEB68(1)` when the timer is 40, decrements the timer, returns `false` while positive, and `true` once the timer expires.

Implemented `itEvYoshiEgg_Logic42_DmgReceived`: early-outs if already processed (`evyoshiegg.xDD4 != 0`) or if `ip->xC9C` is below `itEvYoshiEgg_DatAttrs.x0`; otherwise spawns effect `1231` twice with local scale `1.5f`, calls `Item_8026AE84(ip, 244, 0x7F, 0x40)`, and transitions through a helper.

Added inline damage-transition helper that hides the model with `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll(..., JOBJ_HIDDEN)`, calls `it_802756D0`, zeros X/Y velocity, sets `evyoshiegg.xDD4 = 1` and `evyoshiegg.xDD8 = 60`, calls `it_8026B3A8`, and changes to state 5 with `Item_80268E5C(..., ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE)`.
Replaced prior placeholder use of `it->xDD4_itemVar.bombhei.xDD4` in EvYoshiEgg spawned logic with `it->xDD4_itemVar.evyoshiegg.xDD4`.
Renamed `it_8029B0C8` to `itEvYoshiEgg_Spawn` and `it_3F14_Logic42_DmgReceived` to `itEvYoshiEgg_Logic42_DmgReceived` across symbols, headers, and logic-table references.
Updated `.sdata2` symbols at `0x804DCC88`, `0x804DCC8C`, and `0x804DCC90` from global `it_804DCCxx` names to local labels `@153`, `@154`, and `@195`.
smart_moves: Introduced item-specific `itEvYoshiEgg_ItemVars` instead of continuing to borrow the `bombhei` union member; this improves semantic accuracy while preserving the shared `xDD4_itemVar` layout.
Matched both code and data: the PR did not stop at function bodies, but also added the EvYoshiEgg `ItemStateTable`, which the bot reported as a new `.data` match.
Kept symbol map, header prototypes, live logic table, and preserved/commented table reference in sync when renaming the damage callback.
Used an item-prefixed name for behavior that had been hidden behind a file/section-derived name, making ownership clear: `itEvYoshiEgg_Logic42_DmgReceived` instead of `it_3F14_Logic42_DmgReceived`.
Localized compiler-owned float constants in `symbols.txt` once the C source owned the constants, avoiding artificial global data names.
decomp_lessons: When an item file reaches matching status, update `configure.py` only after all dependent pieces are aligned: C implementation, state tables, symbols, headers, and logic-table call sites.
For `Item::xDD4_itemVar` work, add a proper per-item union member in `types.h` and a typedef in `itCommonItems.h`; avoid using another item’s member as a placeholder once ownership is known.
Item matching often requires reconstructing state-table `.data`, not just replacing `/// #function` placeholders with C code.
If a callback name came from a broad source bucket like `it_3F14`, rename it to the owning item prefix once the logic table and behavior identify it.
Unknown fields can remain offset-style (`x0`, `x4`, `xDD4`, `xDD8`) while still improving structure ownership and matching; do not over-name semantics without stronger evidence.
Timer/latch item behavior can be expressed with item vars: here `xDD4` acts as a processed flag and `xDD8` as a countdown in the matched source, though exact semantic names were not proven in the PR.
naming_conventions: Global identified event Yoshi Egg functions use `itEvYoshiEgg_` prefix, e.g. `itEvYoshiEgg_Spawn` and `itEvYoshiEgg_Logic42_DmgReceived`.
Existing motion callbacks retain the spelling `itEvyoshiegg_UnkMotionN_{Anim,Phys,Coll}`; the PR preserves that convention rather than normalizing all names to `itEvYoshiEgg`.
Logic callbacks use `Logic42_*` suffixes for this item’s table entries, such as `Destroyed`, `Spawned`, `DmgReceived`, and `EnteredAir`.
Per-item structs follow `itEvYoshiEgg_DatAttrs` and `itEvYoshiEgg_ItemVars`; the union member is lowercase `evyoshiegg`.
Unknown struct fields remain offset-style (`x0`, `x4`, `xDD4`, `xDD8`) where the diff does not prove names.
Local float/data symbols in `symbols.txt` use compiler-style `@NNN` labels when they are not true globals.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The source uses direct `SpawnItem` field assignments, chained zeroing of velocity components, and parent-gobj aliasing (`x4_parent_gobj2 = x0_parent_gobj`), which is the kind of exact statement shaping commonly needed for item spawn matches.
A `static inline` helper isolates the damage-received state transition while allowing the emitted code to inline into `itEvYoshiEgg_Logic42_DmgReceived`.
The damage function keeps early-return guard order explicit: already-processed check first, damage-threshold check second, then effect/sound-or-item-event calls and state transition.
The matched effect setup uses a local `f32 val = 1.5f` and two identical `efSync_Spawn(1231, ...)` calls; this correlates with the `.sdata2` constant-symbol cleanup in the same PR.
The state table entry order and exact callbacks, including a `NULL` collision callback in one state, were added to recover `.data` matching.

Evidence for unused-local matching intent is weak, but the diff includes unused local declarations such as `itEvYoshiEgg_ItemVars* attrs;` in spawn and `f32* unused;` in damage-received; do not remove similar oddities without checking codegen.
review_feedback: No human PR body, reviews, or review comments were present in the slice.

decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 progress: matched code 55.29% (+0.01%, +548 bytes), linked code 31.82% (+0.04%, +1584 bytes), matched data 34.60% (+0.01%, +112 bytes), linked data 29.18% (+0.01%, +112 bytes).
Bot-listed new matches: `itEvYoshiEgg_Logic42_DmgReceived` (+260 bytes), `itEvYoshiEgg_Spawn` (+192 bytes), `.data` (+96 bytes), `itEvyoshiegg_UnkMotion5_Anim` (+96 bytes), and `.sdata2` (+4 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Search for other item files still borrowing the wrong `xDD4_itemVar` union member as a placeholder.
Find remaining `it_3F14_Logic42_*` callbacks that may deserve item-specific prefixes.
Audit `itEvYoshiEgg_DatAttrs.x0`/`x4` and `itEvYoshiEgg_ItemVars.xDD4`/`xDD8` once stronger semantic evidence is available.
Compare `itEvyoshiegg_UnkMotion*` spelling against `itEvYoshiEgg_*` if future naming-cleanup policy allows consistency changes.
Look for other nonmatching item objects where adding the `ItemStateTable` `.data` may unlock matching/linking progress.
